annotations.description: The engine-analysis subsystem is refactored so each detection-engine context owns mutable analysis state (file handles, compiled regex objects, buffer tracking, fast-pattern statistics, and related bookkeeping), multi-tenant log paths incorporate each engine’s tenant prefix, legacy per-function setup/cleanup entry points are hidden behind the unified public lifecycle API specified in the task interface, call sites in the loader and related compilation units are updated, two named bugs (missing PCRE teardown and narrow content length type) are fixed, and DetectEngineCtx_ gains the interface-specified analysis pointer and classification/reference regex fields.

title: 1.1: Moves all four prompt-listed state categories (output file handles, compiled PCRE objects, analyzer item tracking, fast pattern statistics) off file-scoped statics into per-DetectEngineCtx storage including struct EngineAnalysisCtx_ pointer field ea

annotations.description: Mutable engine-analysis state that previously lived at file scope in src/detect-engine-analyzer.c is owned per DetectEngineCtx. DetectEngineCtx_ includes the opaque per-engine analysis pointer struct EngineAnalysisCtx_ *ea described in the interface, initialized to NULL at engine creation and populated by the unified setup entry point when analysis is enabled, so concurrent engine contexts cannot clobber one another’s in-memory state.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch removes file-scoped FILE pointers, PCRE pointers, log path buffer, pattern stats array, analyzer maps, and related globals from src/detect-engine-analyzer.c and attaches equivalent state through de_ctx->ea in src/detect.h and the analyzer implementation.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires each DetectEngineCtx to own its own analysis state. [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The supplied interface requires struct EngineAnalysisCtx_ *ea on DetectEngineCtx_. [3: Logical Necessity] Without per-context storage and a stable anchor field, multi-tenant setups still share globals. [5: Conclusion] This rubric checks ownership and the interface-mandated linkage field, not internal struct member names inside EngineAnalysisCtx_.

title: 1.2: Introduces both unified public lifecycle functions (SetupEngineAnalysis, CleanupEngineAnalysis) on DetectEngineCtx in src/detect-engine-analyzer.h

annotations.description: src/detect-engine-analyzer.h declares void SetupEngineAnalysis(struct DetectEngineCtx_ *de_ctx, bool *fp_analysis, bool *rule_analysis) and void CleanupEngineAnalysis(struct DetectEngineCtx_ *de_ctx) with behaviors described in the interface: setup allocates the per-engine analysis record on de_ctx->ea, derives a file prefix from de_ctx->config_prefix, enables the configured analyzers, writes the two booleans, and frees the record when nothing is enabled; cleanup tears down open resources, frees compiled percent-encoding regex objects, frees file_prefix and analyzer_items when allocated, frees the analysis record, and clears de_ctx->ea.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch defines and declares SetupEngineAnalysis and CleanupEngineAnalysis with the interface signatures; src/detect-engine-loader.c switches to these entry points.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires a unified public setup/cleanup API on DetectEngineCtx. [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The supplied interface fixes the public names and signatures for these two functions. [3: Logical Necessity] Callers must share one supported lifecycle surface. [5: Conclusion] This rubric encodes the interface contract rather than optional naming.

title: 1.3: Stops exporting all six legacy helpers (SetupFPAnalyzer, CleanupFPAnalyzer, SetupRuleAnalyzer, CleanupRuleAnalyzer, PerCentEncodingSetup, PerCentEncodingMatch) from src/detect-engine-analyzer.h

annotations.description: The six functions listed in the prompt are no longer declared for external use in src/detect-engine-analyzer.h. They may be defined as static in src/detect-engine-analyzer.c or otherwise hidden, but translation units outside the analyzer cannot call them directly except through the unified public API.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch removes the six legacy prototypes from src/detect-engine-analyzer.h and marks the implementations static inside src/detect-engine-analyzer.c.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt lists these six functions as must be internalized behind the unified API. [3: Logical Necessity] Leaving them public allows bypassing per-engine initialization. [5: Conclusion] This rubric checks encapsulation using the prompt’s explicit symbol list.

title: 1.4: Routes rule analysis output, fast-pattern analysis output, JSON side output, failure logging, and percent-encoding checks through per-engine analysis state

annotations.description: Analyzer routines that write rules_fast_pattern.txt, rules_analysis.txt, or rules.json data, emit EngineAnalysisRulesFailure diagnostics, or run percent-encoding detection obtain FILE handles, scratch buffers, and compiled regex objects from the active DetectEngineCtx’s analysis record (de_ctx->ea) instead of file-scoped statics.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch threads DetectEngineCtx through helpers and replaces fprintf paths that previously used static FILE pointers in src/detect-engine-analyzer.c, including JSON and failure paths.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt’s motivation is cross-tenant clobbering of shared handles. [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The interface states EngineAnalysisRulesFailure must use de_ctx->ea for output selection. [5: Conclusion] This rubric verifies behavioral routing, not whether two or one FILE streams are used internally.

title: 1.5: Prefixes all three engine-analysis filenames (rules_fast_pattern.txt, rules_analysis.txt, rules.json) using each engine’s config_prefix when non-empty

annotations.description: Path construction for each standard engine-analysis artifact under the configured log directory incorporates the engine’s config_prefix-derived file prefix when non-empty so tenants do not overwrite one another; when the prefix is empty, legacy unprefixed filenames remain acceptable.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch derives a per-engine prefix from DetectEngineCtx::config_prefix and applies it when opening rules_fast_pattern.txt, rules_analysis.txt, and rules.json in src/detect-engine-analyzer.c.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires log files to incorporate config_prefix for multi-tenant isolation. [3: Logical Necessity] Skipping any one of the three filenames would still allow collisions for that artifact class. [5: Conclusion] Separator characters between prefix and basename are implementation-defined.

title: 1.6: Updates all three named production files (src/detect-engine-loader.c, src/detect-engine-build.c, src/detect-flowbits.c) to use the unified analyzer lifecycle

annotations.description: The three files named in the prompt are edited so loader setup/cleanup uses SetupEngineAnalysis and CleanupEngineAnalysis, any remaining references to legacy setup/cleanup entry points are removed, and extern declarations of shared analysis flags stay consistent with the loader definition.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch replaces legacy setup/cleanup calls in src/detect-engine-loader.c, updates extern declarations in src/detect-engine-build.c and src/detect-flowbits.c, and passes DetectEngineCtx into EngineAnalysisRulesFailure at the loader call site.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly lists these three files for updates. [3: Logical Necessity] Stale prototypes or mismatched extern types break compilation or introduce undefined behavior. [5: Conclusion] Incidental edits in those files are acceptable when required for consistency.

title: 1.7: Frees both compiled percent-encoding PCRE objects (percent_re, percent_re_study) during unified analyzer cleanup

annotations.description: CleanupEngineAnalysis (or its delegated cleanup path) invokes the project’s PCRE free helpers on the percent-encoding compile and study objects allocated during setup so they are not leaked when an engine context is destroyed or analysis is torn down.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch issues pcre_free calls for the percent-encoding compile and study objects inside the analyzer cleanup sequence in src/detect-engine-analyzer.c.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt names percent_re and percent_re_study as leaked today. [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The interface’s CleanupEngineAnalysis description mandates freeing those objects. [5: Conclusion] This rubric checks resource release, not which static helper performs the free.

title: 1.8: Widens PerCentEncodingMatch content length beyond uint8_t without narrowing callers

annotations.description: The internal percent-encoding match routine uses an unsigned integral type at least 16 bits wide for the content length parameter so values above 255 are not truncated before calling the regex executor, and internal call sites pass the full DetectContentData (or equivalent) length using that contract.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch widens the internal helper’s length parameter and updates its call sites in src/detect-engine-analyzer.c.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly calls out uint8_t truncation. [2: Implicit Code Base Constraint] HTTP buffer contents can exceed 255 bytes. [5: Conclusion] Wider integer types remain acceptable if they preserve correctness.

title: 1.9: Relocates both hash tables (class_conf_ht, reference_conf_ht) and adds all four interface regex fields (class_conf_regex, class_conf_regex_study, reference_conf_regex, reference_conf_regex_study) on DetectEngineCtx_

annotations.description: In src/detect.h, DetectEngineCtx_ matches the interface: class_conf_ht and reference_conf_ht move from their historical location near sc_sig_order_funcs to immediately after sm_types_silent_error, and the four new pcre / pcre_extra fields with the interface-specified names are declared contiguously with those hash table pointers so configuration parsing state stays grouped.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch removes the hash table pointers from their old struct region and reinserts them after sm_types_silent_error alongside the four new regex pointer fields.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires pcre/pcre_extra fields grouped with the two hash tables. [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The supplied interface mandates the relocation, grouping, and exact field identifiers. [5: Conclusion] This rubric encodes the published DetectEngineCtx_ layout contract.

title: 1.10: Changes both analysis flag variables (rule_engine_analysis_set, fp_engine_analysis_set) to bool with matching extern declarations

annotations.description: src/detect-engine-loader.c defines rule_engine_analysis_set and the file-scoped fp_engine_analysis_set as bool, writes bool results from SetupEngineAnalysis into them, and every extern declaration in src/detect-engine-build.c and src/detect-flowbits.c uses bool to match the definition.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch switches both variables to bool in src/detect-engine-loader.c and updates the extern lines in src/detect-engine-build.c and src/detect-flowbits.c.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The supplied interface mandates the int-to-bool conversion for both variables and consistent extern declarations. [3: Logical Necessity] Mismatched extern types are undefined behavior in C. [5: Conclusion] This rubric enforces the interface’s type contract.

title: 1.11: Threads const struct DetectEngineCtx_ *de_ctx through EngineAnalysisRulesFailure declaration, definition, and loader call site

annotations.description: EngineAnalysisRulesFailure’s prototype matches the interface: void EngineAnalysisRulesFailure(const struct DetectEngineCtx_ *de_ctx, char *line, char *file, int lineno). The implementation reads per-engine analysis output handles through de_ctx->ea, and src/detect-engine-loader.c passes the active DetectEngineCtx pointer at the call site.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch updates the header declaration, function definition in src/detect-engine-analyzer.c, and the detect-engine-loader.c invocation.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The supplied interface mandates this signature change so failures log through per-engine state. [3: Logical Necessity] Without the parameter, failures cannot target the correct tenant output. [5: Conclusion] This rubric locks the published API change.

annotations.description: Optional documentation or comment updates that explain the unified analyzer lifecycle, per-engine ownership, or multi-tenant log naming may be added when they materially help maintainers; the functional prompt does not require standalone documentation files.

Obsolete file-scoped declarations, unused buffers, and other dead artifacts left behind after migrating state into per-engine storage should be removed when safe, reducing confusion about which variables are authoritative.

title: 3.1: Removes replaced file-scoped static declarations in src/detect-engine-analyzer.c

annotations.description: After migrating engine-analysis state, superseded static FILE pointers, static PCRE pointers, static scratch path buffers, static statistics arrays, static buffer maps, and other globals in src/detect-engine-analyzer.c that duplicated the new per-engine record are deleted instead of being left as dead code.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch deletes the superseded static/global definitions at the top of src/detect-engine-analyzer.c once the per-engine struct owns them.
annotations.justification: [4: Qualitative Requirement] Removing dead globals prevents accidental reuse. [3: Logical Necessity] Duplicate definitions invite inconsistent edits. [5: Conclusion] Partial cleanup still satisfies the positive intent; complete removal is ideal.

annotations.description: Harmful outcomes for this task include touching automated tests despite an explicit freeze, or preserving legacy public analyzer entry points that defeat encapsulation. Verifiers in this category pass when the harmful pattern is detected in the candidate diff.

title: 4.1: Modifies automated test sources or Suricata unittest harness wiring outside production detector sources

annotations.description: Detect additions or edits under unittest harness files, generated test registrations, or other test-only sources that are not part of production Suricata sources. Pass this verifier when such test changes appear; fail when the production diff excludes test-only paths.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch limits edits to production sources under src/ (analyzer, loader, build, flowbits, detect.h) and does not touch test directories.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt forbids modifying any test files or testing logic. [5: Conclusion] This negative verifier flags violations of that explicit constraint.

title: 4.2: Keeps all six legacy helpers (SetupFPAnalyzer, CleanupFPAnalyzer, SetupRuleAnalyzer, CleanupRuleAnalyzer, PerCentEncodingSetup, PerCentEncodingMatch) declared in src/detect-engine-analyzer.h

annotations.description: Detect continued callable prototypes in src/detect-engine-analyzer.h for any of the six legacy functions after the refactor (excluding comments). Pass this verifier when any prototype remains published; fail when the header exports only the unified interface plus the non-legacy analysis helpers.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch removes all six legacy prototypes from src/detect-engine-analyzer.h while adding SetupEngineAnalysis, CleanupEngineAnalysis, and the revised EngineAnalysisRulesFailure declaration.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt requires internalizing these helpers behind the unified API. [3: Logical Necessity] Published legacy prototypes let callers bypass per-engine initialization. [5: Conclusion] This negative verifier complements the positive encapsulation checks.
